notes: all combat numbers are simulator output on unverified constants
checkpoint levels are provisional (no level timeline in the match feed)
evidence deltas carry finished-inventory survivorship bias

this build stacks over +100% attack speed; the sim now caps attacks/sec at the stated 3.0 (Cursed Ring tooltip), so sustained-DPS is no longer uncapped — the exact cap interaction still merits a practice-mode check
the sim opens on Viper, which the field rarely builds (0.2% pick) — a sim-only call; compare the meta cores below
the sim build reproduces only 33% of the field's core for GRIM.exe (it never builds Lightning Hawk, Dust Devil) — some of those items ride mechanics the sim doesn't model yet

coachLine: Sim-optimal for sustained DPS: first spike lands around minute 12 (Viper), third item by minute 24. Take Demiurge: +27% on this kit’s main output at minute 15.

all.name: Marrow
all.note: Marrow (Major): missing-health-dependent omnivamp; no sustain-uptime model yet

all.name: Nihil
all.note: Nihil (Major): accrual rate depends on combat uptime (how long Doom persists per fight), which the closed-form sim cannot observe

all.name: Aion
all.note: Aion (Major): economy effect; valuable but outside combat objectives (revisit with gold curves)

all.name: Exarch
all.note: Exarch (Major): buffs the HEAL TARGET (usually an ally) — team-side value outside the single-hero sim; self-heal uptime depends on heal cadence vs the 4s window

metaBuilds.title: Crit Skirmisher
metaBuilds.whyLine: Strongest meta core for 10s rotation damage, online around minute 25 — about 851 damage over a 10s rotation in the sim.
metaBuilds.optimizer: Optimizer sees +73% more 10s rotation damage: try Viper over Lightning Hawk (sim-only — the popular pick may carry utility the sim can't see; test it).

metaBuilds.title: Crit Skirmisher
metaBuilds.whyLine: Strongest meta core for 10s rotation damage, online around minute 25 — about 888 damage over a 10s rotation in the sim.
metaBuilds.optimizer: Optimizer sees +66% more 10s rotation damage: try Viper over Lightning Hawk (sim-only — the popular pick may carry utility the sim can't see; test it).

metaBuilds.title: Crit Burst
metaBuilds.whyLine: Strongest meta core for one-combo burst, online around minute 25 — about 1,110 damage in one combo in the sim.
metaBuilds.optimizer: Optimizer sees +17% more one-combo burst: try Viper over Plasma Blade (sim-only — the popular pick may carry utility the sim can't see; test it).

matchups.gameplan: You hold the kill-threat edge wherever one exists: play front-foot, but respect jungle attention. Sparrow ramps on auto-attacks — pressure their positioning before they itemize, and armor blunts them later.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Warden's Faith instead of Imperator: their 3s all-in loses 35% of its bite; you give up 31% of your damage.

matchups.gameplan: Your window is early: force trades before their core completes, and do not coin-flip fights past the crossover. Murdock deals area damage — spread out so one cast can't catch the group.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Raiment of Renewal instead of Imperator: their 3s all-in loses 24% of its bite; you give up 31% of your damage.

matchups.gameplan: You hold the kill-threat edge wherever one exists: play front-foot, but respect jungle attention. Eden ramps on auto-attacks — pressure them before they itemize, and armor blunts them later.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Warden's Faith instead of Imperator: their 3s all-in loses 35% of its bite; you give up 31% of your damage.

matchups.gameplan: You hold the kill-threat edge wherever one exists: play front-foot, but respect jungle attention. Drongo deals area damage — spread out so one cast can't catch the group.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Raiment of Renewal instead of Imperator: their 3s all-in loses 24% of its bite; you give up 31% of your damage.

matchups.gameplan: You hold the kill-threat edge wherever one exists: play front-foot, but respect jungle attention. Revenant ramps on auto-attacks — pressure their positioning before they itemize, and mixed resist blunts them later.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Raiment of Renewal instead of Imperator: their 3s all-in loses 24% of its bite; you give up 31% of your damage.

matchups.gameplan: You hold the kill-threat edge wherever one exists: play front-foot, but respect jungle attention. Wraith ramps on auto-attacks — pressure their positioning before they itemize, and mixed resist blunts them later.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Raiment of Renewal instead of Imperator: their 3s all-in loses 24% of its bite; you give up 31% of your damage.

laneSteer.provenance: “System Restore” ⇒ sustain; the sim can’t model this augment — steered by the declared playstyle + field evidence — field carry 50.9% over 174,696 games, magnitude not simulated.
